obligation_text: The risk management measures shall give due consideration to the effects and possible interactions resulting from the combined application of the requirements set out in this Section, with a view to minimising risks more effectively while achieving an appropriate balance in implementing the measures to fulfil those requirements.
scoring_text: Ability to assess interaction effects between multiple AI system requirements and design balanced risk management measures that address combined risks while maintaining regulatory compliance across all applicable requirements.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Ability to assess interaction effects between multiple AI system requirements and design balanced risk management measures that address combined risks while maintaining regulatory compliance across all applicable requirements.
reference_document_element_description: Conduct AI system risk classification under the EU AI Act, documenting risk category determination, applicable requirements identification, and conformity assessment pathway selection.
comments: EU AI Act Art. 9(4) (obligation) superset of WIDAI RC domain. RC-T-012 is the strongest match (STS: 0.581, cosine: 0.719). Secondary: AG-T-011 (AG, cosine: 0.711), AG-K-008 (AG, cosine: 0.703).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the regulatory requirement) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.8/10 (raw 0.581). Shared vocabulary: [ai, applicable, requirements, risk, system]. EU AI Act competency implication terms [ability, across, address, assess, balanced, combined] vs WIDAI terms [act, assessment, category, classification, conduct, conformity] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.877 (P=0.868, R=0.887). Balanced P/R — the 28-word competency implication and 22-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs (mean ~0.007) because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.8%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the EU AI Act element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.719 > STS 0.581 (gap +0.138). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [ai, applicable, requirements, risk, system] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 14.6%. Shared tokens: [ai, applicable, requirements, risk, system]. EU AI Act-only: [ability, across, address, assess, balanced, combined]. WIDAI-only: [act, assessment, category, classification, conduct, conformity].
